compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: P1 (Yellow Pot) is present but appears devoid of visible foliage. P3 (Black Pot with Rabbit) contains a single leaf specimen. P2/P4 (Black Pot) contains a single leaf specimen. The registry is largely inconsistent with the current biological state.

inventory_reconciliation.p4_silver_guest: Present (Single leaf, shared with P2 pot)

plant_audit.p2_p4: Single leaf remaining, showing signs of chlorosis or structural weakness.
plant_audit.p3: Single leaf remaining, anchored by rabbit figurine.

biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in P3/P4 pots, likely used as calcium amendment or mulch.
biome_observations.anomalies: Significant loss of biomass across all specimens over the 5-day period.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial state showed two leaves in P3/P4 area.
temporal_deltas.t1_to_current: Severe reduction in visible plant matter; P1 remains barren.

vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is in a state of advanced decline. The Pothos (P3) and the Silver Guest (P4) are the only remaining biological entities, each reduced to a single leaf. The String of Nickels (P1) and Mexican Mint (P2) appear to have suffered total systemic loss. The presence of eggshells suggests an attempt at soil enrichment, but the current visual evidence indicates a failure to thrive, likely due to environmental stress or lack of sufficient light/nutrients.
